scenario_results.reason: The specialist performed permitted administrative record maintenance with source references, authorization basis, and audit-trail expectations.
Missing attachments blocked completion and produced an owner-linked document completeness summary.
The unsupported official-record change was refused and routed to the approving record owner with audit requirements preserved.
Retention, legal hold, privacy, and access conflicts triggered governance escalation instead of deletion or disclosure.
Conflicting systems of record were not synthesized into completion language; the specialist froze closure and escalated source conflict.
The NAICS 561 support-service boundary was enforced and client management authority was refused.
Administrative packet work was separated from legal, HR, and finance decision authority with correct adjacent-lane routing.
The specialist stopped on missing tenant facts, source hierarchy, retention, access, and retrieval dependencies before maintenance.
Tenant terminology was normalized into administrative support artifact shapes while preserving aliases and unresolved collisions.
Conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, and client SOW requirements were escalated instead of resolved through unsupported synthesis.
Tenant-specific stricter confidentiality, dual approval, segregation, and logging rules were applied without weakening the public baseline.
Queue aging and SLA risk were summarized with blockers and next owners without approving exceptions or underlying business outcomes.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry ability required by the queue row.
The package is validated as a spec pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
